title: Digital Marketing - Acquisition - Dutch Speaker
description: Due to continued expansion and growth, Accenture is currently recruiting for a Partner Manager to drive the rollout of a unique digital marketing advertising solution to EU markets. We are partnering with Pinterest to ensure businesses are set up for success on the platform. You will support new clients from the very start of their Pinterest journey and help to educate, inspire and help advertisers define the best type of campaigns to drive results and achieve their goals. This is an opportunity to work with one of the fastest\-growing players in social media, with the support of a leading global professional services company. Our high\-performing Pinterest team has a proud track record. We maintain a positive, high\-performing team environment by looking out for each other, cherishing diversity and taking time to stop and celebrate success together regularly. **Responsibilities** * We put the power in your hands; to identify, research and contact potential new partner businesses that you believe Pinterest’s unique business proposition could bring value to, delivering personalised product pitches. * Setting the advertiser up for success before passing over to a colleague in Account management to be scaled * You will also work with businesses and brands that have already started advertising on the platform to understand their strategic and tactical goals and co\-create the right marketing plan to achieve them. * Gain an in\-depth understanding of partners' business goals in their respective markets, offering educated insights to help them to succeed. * Developing and growing long term relationships as the ‘face’ \& first point of contact for your clients. * Work toward fixed KPIs \& a quarterly sales target. Work collaboratively with the wider sales team in order to share (and gain) valuable knowledge on your * . \#LI\-EU **Basic Qualifications** * Completed High School Diploma (Irish Leaving cert or equivalent) **Qualifications** * At least one year of experience in a target\-driven, customer\-facing role (sales is preferred). * In\-depth, demonstrated knowledge of digital marketing, including understanding of metrics such as CPA, CPC, CPM and ROAS. * Demonstrated experience using analytical approaches to solving business problems. * Demonstrable ability to train and educate advertisers on solutions including how to create and optimise campaigns. * Demonstrable ability to understand, validate, and overcome customer objections by crafting a compelling marketing plan to use Pinterest’s platform \& functionality to achieve advertisers goals. * Ability to learn quickly and maintain current, comprehensive knowledge of the client’s products and services. * Tech\-savvy with an affinity for innovative and emerging technology. * Self\-reliant, adaptable, decisive, and professional; able to effectively multi\-task in a dynamic environment. * Proven experience in working toward quarterly goals, in a KPI\-driven environment. **What do we offer:** * Hybrid role (3 days working from Office and 2 days working from home) * Monday to Friday * Health Insurance * Social team events (indoor and outdoor) * 20 days annual leave \+ 2 Company days \+ 2 volunteers. * Recognition points which you can spend on shopping or vouchers. *Please also note that Accenture is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all sections of society and does not discriminate on grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnic or national origin, disability, age, citizenship, marital, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation, or gender identity.* *Please advise if you have any particular requirements, so we can look into arranging reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process. You can contact us at* *accessIErecruitment@accenture.com*

title: Electrical Engineering Specialist
description: **Senior Electrical Owners Engineer (Engineering Design Services / Construction Transmission Projects (Substation, HV cable and Overhead Line Design)** Reporting to: **Client Engineering Manager** Location: **Dublin/ Cork: Hybrid** (candidates can be located around Ireland, with pay expenses for travel) Type: **Permanent Or Contractor** (Initial Contract Duration: 12\+ months) Job Description This role sits within the **Engineering \& Asset Management** team and is responsible for delivering the **Senior Electrical Owners Engineer** s function across **Transmission Projects.** The successful candidate will ensure that projects are developed in line with client’s statutory obligations, standards and policies. The **Client Engineering Function** includes **Design Reviews, Site Inspections,** and **Pre\-Commissioning** and **Commissioning Inspections** for acceptance on both contestable and non\-contestable **Transmission Projects,** ensuring compliance with functional specifications and standards. A key aspect of the role is the preparation and issue of functional and **Technical Specifications** to customers connecting to the **Transmission System** , together with ongoing engagement to resolve technical queries. This is essential to ensure that all **connections** meet **Ireland’s Electricity Transmission System Operator** **(TSO)** specifications, operational requirements, and relevant national and international standards. These customer connections are strategically important to **Ireland’s** **Renewable Energy** ambitions and to supporting sustainable economic growth. The successful candidate will also perform the **Senior Electrical Owners Engineer** s role for **Transmission Reinforcement** and network expansion projects delivered by **Ireland’s Electricity Transmission System Operator** **(TSO)** and **Distribution System/Networks Operator (DNO)** The ideal candidate will bring strong expertise in **Substation** , **HV cable** and **Overhead Line Design** , together with the ability to assess **Project Designs, Construction Activities** and **Commissioning Phases** for compliance with standards and requirements for **High Voltage Alternating Current projects** . The role also requires the effective management of technical queries throughout project delivery, ensuring they are addressed promptly and in accordance with **Ireland’s Electricity Transmission System Operator** **(TSO)** standards and policies. A key part of the position is the application of sound technical judgement and problem\-solving capability to ensure that appropriate technical standards are consistently achieved across projects. Key Responsibilities Ensure that technical queries raised during projects are managed in accordance with **Ireland’s Electricity Transmission System Operator** **(TSO)** standards and policies and are resolved efficiently. Ensure that projects are delivered in a manner that meets **Ireland’s Electricity Transmission System Operator** **(TSO)** requirements. Review and assess submitted detailed designs against **Ireland’s Electricity Transmission System Operator** **(TSO)** functional requirements, standard designs, and relevant national and international standards. Undertake on\-site inspection and monitoring of **Construction Progress** to ensure compliance with company standards, approved **Detailed Designs** , and relevant national and international standards. Undertake **On\-Site Inspection** and monitoring of **Pre\-Commissioning** activities to ensure compliance with company standards and relevant national and international standards. Verify that all **Transmission System** requirements have been satisfied to enable **Commissioning** tests to proceed. Ensure that developments are commissioned by the TAO in compliance with their requirements through the following activities: Prepare and issue **Commissioning Instructions.** Monitor and audit **Commissioning** activities against **Commissioning Instructions** and relevant national and international standards. **Lead Engagement** with customer and **Stakeholder Technical Consultants,** as required, to resolve issues arising at different stages of the project. Coordinate with peers to support business priorities, manage emerging issues, provide technical guidance, and act as an escalation point for the **Client Engineering Manager.** Manage project interactions with **Client Engineering Vendors, Customers,** and internal **Stakeholders.** Provide technical direction and support across the organisation on assigned projects, including guidance to team members and junior staff, and act as a team representative when required. Ensure completion of Stage Gate Reviews for all customer projects to confirm compliance with functional specifications, and capture and share lessons learned following project energisation. Contribute to reviews and support the introduction of new tools and technologies, as well as the development and implementation of procedures required for the effective delivery of **Transmission Projects.** Lead engagement and coordination with **Distribution System/Networks Operator (DNO)** , including meetings, reporting, relationship management, and general communications relevant to the client engineering role. Represent in Factory Acceptance Test for **HV equipment** part of his projects scope as required. Support and contribute to project reviews, assessments, and audits conducted by internal and external parties, as required. Prepare and present reports and project approval papers for the Executive, the **Grid Infrastructure Projects Committee,** and the Board, as required, within strict deadlines. Prepare relay settings request and Energisation Instruction request for the assigned projects. This request is sent operation to produce these documents. Knowledge, Skills and Experience * **Degree in Electrical or Electronic Engineering, with a minimum of 6–8 years’** experience in the delivery of **HVAC transmission assets.** * Demonstrated expertise in **Transmission Substation Design** , including experience assessing **Onshore Substation Designs** for compliance with standards at **110/220/400 kV.** * Excellent communication skills, with a strong emphasis on high\-quality report writing and the ability to communicate technical issues clearly and effectively. * Experience working with and implementing **CENELEC standards** relevant to **Substation Design, Overhead Lines,** and **Power Cable Systems.** * Experience ensuring that **TAO, Generator, Demand and IPP customers** are provided with the required functional and **Technical Standards.** * Experience in carrying out on\-site inspection and monitoring of **Construction and Commissioning** activities to ensure compliance and readiness for **Connection** to the **Transmission System.** * Experience in preparing and issuing **Transmission Design Drawings** , including **Single Line Diagrams, Substation Layout Drawings, Protection Schematics** , and interlocking documents. * Proven ability to track and monitor key tasks, take ownership of deliverables, and lead and influence others towards a common vision while working collaboratively across teams. * A proactive and engaging approach, with the drive and enthusiasm to contribute to delivery of the company strategy. * Strong understanding of stakeholder and industry requirements relating to the **Transmission System** and associated developments. * Ability to coach and mentor colleagues to support the delivery of a high\-quality client engineering service. * Proven ability to provide technical input to **Project Managers** , while tracking key tasks and taking ownership of technical deliverables. * Excellent interpersonal, analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Strong commercial awareness, combined with effective **Vendor Management Capability** . * **Experience Managing Consultancy Engineering Vendors** to ensure effective and efficient **Delivery Of Engineering Services** , including design review and site monitoring. * Full driving licence. If you have a minimum experience requirement is **6\-8 years – and are an Electrical or Electronic Engineer** based any where in Ireland and have a readiness to travel to office locations in **Dublin or Cork,** we would like to hear from you **hello@enexa\-partners.com**

title: Field Service Engineer (m/w/d)
description: Working at EBARA Precision Machinery Europe (EPME) means you are connected to the top 20 micro\-chip manufacturers. The semiconductor manufacturing market continues to grow. Our customers are using high\-precision manufacturing technologies to provide; IoT, cloud servers, silicon technology, 5G, sensors for autonomous vehicles, flat panel displays, smartphones and tablets. As a part of the EBARA Group with over 19,000 employees worldwide, we drive this technology development. We are a team with more than 200 employees in 8 countries. Shape the future with us! Join us in a perspective\-rich workplace in a growing industry. We are looking for ambitious and passionate candidates who want to push boundaries to drive our customers' success. Join us in a company with a successful culture and team spirit. **THE ROLE** This is a cleanroom\-based Field Service Engineer role, working directly on advanced planar semiconductor equipment — specifically Chemical Mechanical Planarization (CMP) systems. If you are passionate about working hands\-on with the cutting\-edge tools that make modern chip manufacturing possible, this is the role for you. Please note: While EBARA is widely known for vacuum and pump technology, this position sits within our Systems division. You will be working exclusively on CMP tools on the fab floor — not on pumps or vacuum systems. **Your Responsibilities** Our Field Service Engineers provide on\-site field service support through the performance of installations, upgrades, repairs, and maintenance of complex CMP capital equipment in a **cleanroom environment** . Working to a shift pattern of days and night shifts, you will analyse, diagnose, and troubleshoot EPME CMP equipment problems. You will also perform installations, repairs, upgrades as well as preventive maintenance on CMP systems installed at the customer site. You will be in charge of maintaining an assigned installation equipment base. Duties include: * Analyse, diagnose and troubleshoot all hardware and software issues on state\-of\-the\-art CMP semiconductor manufacturing equipment in a cleanroom setting. * Understand and implement factory issued hardware and software upgrades. * Ability to read and understand technical drawings, electrical schematics and P\&ID drawings, and utilise these in the resolution of complex system issues. * The successful candidate will take an active role in the management and control of the spare parts that we supply to the customer in support of the CMP Systems business. * Report out to both the onsite customer and local management on weekly basis detailing system performance and ongoing activities. **Skills And Qualifications** * Higher Certificate/Apprenticeship (NFQ level 6\) in a technical field with a minimum of 3 years’ experience in a HVM environment or an Ordinary Bachelor’s Degree (NFQ level 7\) in a technical field with a minimum of 1 year experience. * Extensive experience in troubleshooting mechanical, electrical and software (PLC) systems is desirable. * Must be able to demonstrate effectively and clearly the ability to deliver clear and concise information to both the internal and external customers. * Eligible to live and work in Ireland. **YOUR PROFILE** * You will be proactive, highly motivated and a self\-starter. * You will be able to work without direct instruction or supervision taking ownership for tasks and activities associated with our systems business. * You will have a can do/will do attitude towards customer requests to help to improve equipment performance in the field. * You will demonstrate the ability to work both alone and within a team environment, ensuring that projects and assignments are followed through to completion within a customer defined schedule. * You will have the ability to follow detailed work instruction, and execute same to a high level of professional workmanship, with high attention to detail. * You will be willing to work shift which includes nights and weekends. * The role is 100% onsite in a cleanroom fab environment — you must be based in Ireland. * You will be willing to travel internationally for training purposes from time to time as required. **What We Offer** Join our team and unlock a world of opportunities! We offer a competitive salary and benefits package, including a competitive base salary, shift allowance, discretionary bonus, contributory pension, and medical cover, which even extends to your family. But that's not all! We invest in our employees' growth through individualised and comprehensive training programs, both on the job and beyond. You'll have access to continuous learning and development opportunities to further enhance your skills and career prospects. Plus, as part of our global team, you'll have the chance to travel and train with industry experts across our extensive EBARA network, opening doors to new experiences and professional connections worldwide. Join us and embark on a fulfilling journey of career advancement and personal growth! **Have we attracted your interest?** We look forward to receiving your detailed online application, stating your salary requirements and earliest possible starting date. If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact Zeynep Spinelli at zspinelli@ebara\-pm.eu.

title: Frontend Engineer
description: Gong harnesses the power of AI to transform how revenue teams win. The Gong Revenue AI Operating System unifies data, insights, and workflows into a single, trusted system that observes, guides, and acts alongside the world’s most successful revenue teams. Powered by the Gong Revenue Graph, AI\-powered intelligence, specialized agents, and trusted applications, Gong helps more than 5,000 companies around the world deeply understand their teams and customers, automate critical sales workflows, and close more deals with less effort. For more information, visit www.gong.io. At Gong, you will join a company built on innovative products, ambitious goals, and passionate people. We are shaping the future of revenue intelligence and we want people who are excited to build what comes next. You will work with a team that dreams big, moves fast, and cares deeply about the craft and about each other. Here, transparency and trust are core to how we operate, and every person has the opportunity to make a visible impact. If you want to grow, stretch, and do work that truly matters, Gong is the place to do the best work of your career. **As a Frontend Engineer at Gong, you will...** * Be a major part of Gong's Frontend guild with Frontend developers from all the teams in the company. * Tackle hard technical problems—some at the architecture level, some at the code level, some at the product level—and come up with solutions, balancing practicality and innovation, short and long term. * Be the owner of the features you develop, while collaborating with product and UX teams from thinking and wireframes processes to production. * Be part of our known architecture huddles when we make future decisions about our code and best practices. * Constantly learn new things \- join professional lectures and workshops organized by Gong. In addition, each developer gets extra learning time on behalf of Gong to gain or improve new professional skills. * Be a part of a successful startup technology team that translates our vision into a solid SaaS product that performs, scales, is easy to use, and makes a big impact. **You should apply if you are...** * A software engineer between 3\-5 years of industry experience developing web applications. * Someone who enjoys having responsibility and a sense of ownership of the product you are creating. * Deeply knowledgeable of how JavaScript works and experienced with React (a must!). * Familiarity with backend languages such as Java, Node.js, C\#, and others is an advantage. * Experienced with large scale, cloud\-based web applications. * Proven experience in the architecture of design systems and components. * Preferably someone with a degree in computer science or similar discipline. * An all\-around player, fast\-learner and self\-motivated individual. * An excellent communicator and team player, with strong verbal and written skills in English. * Most importantly, a person who enjoys working independently and within a team of highly skilled peers. **What makes Engineering at Gong unique?** Here at Gong, we trust and empower our employees with ownership to solve complex problems, make the right decisions, and build the best products that create radical impact. We call this “Own. Solve. Impact.” For our engineers, it means it's not just about writing code. You are involved and able to influence the entire process to create the best product. If you are curious to discover Gong's wonderful and challenging world, what are you waiting for? Don’t delay \- fill in your application details. Who knows, maybe there’s a Gongster in you! Perks \& Benefits * We offer Gongsters a variety of private health insurance plans, designed to fit you and your family’s needs. * Wellbeing Fund \- flexible wellness stipend to support a healthy lifestyle. * Mental Health benefits with covered therapy and coaching. * Education \& learning stipend for personal growth and development. * Flexible vacation time to promote a healthy work\-life blend. * Paid parental leave to support you and your family. * Company\-wide recharge days each quarter. **About Us** Gong empowers everyone in revenue teams to improve productivity, increase predictability, and drive revenue growth by deeply understanding customers and business trends; driving impactful decisions and actions. The Gong Revenue AI Platform captures and contextualizes customer interactions, surfaces insights and predictions, and powers actions and workflows that are essential for business success. More than 4,500 companies around the world rely on Gong to unlock their revenue potential. For more information, visit www.gong.io. **We have noticed a rise in recruiting impersonations across the industry, where scammers attempt to access candidates' personal and financial information through fake interviews and offers. All Gong recruiting email communications will always come from the @gong.io domain. Any outreach claiming to be from Gong via other sources should be ignored.** *Gong believes in offering competitive compensation and benefits tailored to the local region. Gong is an equal\-opportunity employer. We believe that diversity is integral to our success, and do not discriminate based on race, colour, religion, age, or any other basis protected by law.* *To review Gong's privacy policy, visit https://www.gong.io/gong\-io\-job\-candidates\-privacy\-notice/ for more details.*

title: Full Stack Engineer, Support Experience
description: Who we are **About Stripe** Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world's largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career. **About The Team** The Support Experience team helps businesses using Stripe succeed by providing high\-quality, contextual, and timely premium support options. Our Dublin\-based team is a startup within Stripe, building a greenfield product—offering both automated, AI\-driven support and improved access to technical experts across Stripe. In addition to building directly for our merchants, we are responsible for building robust internal tooling that empowers Stripe employees in support roles to deliver the best possible experience for our customers. What you'll do As an early hire on the Support Experience team, your role more closely resembles that of a founding engineer at a startup within Stripe. You'll collaborate with peer teams and cross\-functional partners to shape our product vision, build robust systems from scratch, and work directly with our early adopters to onboard them onto our products and gather feedback. **Responsibilities** * Drive projects from problem statement to implementation, taking ownership over everything from business impact to operational excellence * Build high\-quality full\-stack product experiences, demonstrating deep care for users' needs and serving as a steward of crafting great experiences * Develop APIs and services that integrate with the existing Stripe product ecosystem * Partner with teams across Stripe to leverage existing products, systems, and infrastructure * Experiment with and leverage AI to build new products and improve existing workflows Who you are We're looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. **Minimum Requirements** * 2–12\+ years of industry software engineering experience (does not include internships or co\-ops) * Capable of navigating new codebases, languages, and technologies to ship products * Reasoned judgement to unblock yourself and make rapid forward progress in ambiguous domains * Comfort experimenting in a new product space and pivoting based on customer needs * Superb communication skills working with technical and non\-technical stakeholders * User empathy and a customer\-centric approach to problem solving and product development **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience with full\-stack development, particularly using the React framework and Ruby * Track record of building new products from scratch * Demonstrated technical leadership on ambiguous, impactful projects * Desire to learn and grow continuously as an engineer and ship impactful work In\-office expectations Office\-assigned Stripes in most of our locations are currently expected to spend at least 50% of the time in a given month in their local office or with users. This expectation may vary depending on role, team and location. For example, Stripes in Stripe Delivery Center roles in Mexico City, Mexico, Bengaluru, India, and Dublin, Ireland work 100% from the office. Also, some teams have greater in\-office attendance requirements, to appropriately support our users and workflows, which the hiring manager will discuss. This approach helps strike a balance between bringing people together for in\-person collaboration and learning from each other, while supporting flexibility when possible. Pay and benefits The annual salary range for this role in the primary location is €85,000 \- €159,600\. This range may change if you are hired in another location. For sales roles, the range provided is the role’s On Target Earnings (“OTE”) range, meaning that the range includes both the sales commissions/sales bonuses target and annual base salary for the role. This salary range may be inclusive of several career levels at Stripe and will be narrowed during the interview process based on a number of factors, including the candidate’s experience, qualifications, and specific location. Applicants interested in this role and who are not located in the primary location may request the annual salary range for their location during the interview process. Specific benefits and details about what compensation is included in the salary range listed above will vary depending on the applicant’s location and can be discussed in more detail during the interview process. Benefits/additional compensation for this role may include: equity, company bonus or sales commissions/bonuses; retirement plans; health benefits; and wellness stipends.

title: Full-Stack Developer - Architect (Azure & Application Modernisation)
description: ### **Description** **Job Specification** *Role Title*: Senior Full\-Stack Architect (Azure \& Application Modernisation) *Location*: Dublin, Ireland or UK (Hybrid) *Department*: Application Modernisation \& Enterprise Integration **About Codec** Codec is an award\-winning digital transformation company with big growth ambitions. We are an established full\-stack IT consultancy, a local alternative to the Big 4, headquartered in Dublin with offices in Galway, Belfast, London, Poland, and Germany. We are growing rapidly and have crafted a clear vision of the future, developing our own ‘IP’ to complement the Cloud stacks from Microsoft and Oracle. As we expand our Application Modernisation team, we are seeking a "Hands\-On Architect"—someone who can design complex systems on a whiteboard and then sit down to write the core code that powers them. **The Role** This is a hybrid **Architect/Developer** role. You will be part of a cross\-functional team delivering robust, user\-friendly public\-facing portals and enterprise integration hubs. You are the technical standard\-bearer for the squad. While the Principal Architect sets the strategy and the Lead runs the project, you own the **Solution Design**. You will decide how the components fit together, define the coding patterns, and mentor the development team to ensure the vision is executed correctly. You will work heavily with the Microsoft UI stack (Razor/Blazor) or Angular 15\+, backed by Azure Serverless technologies. ### **Key Responsibilities** **Solution Design \& Architecture*** **Technical Definition:** Define the granular architecture for web applications, selecting the right Azure services (Web Apps, Functions, Containers) to meet the requirements. * **Integration Strategy:** Design seamless integrations with back\-end systems, specifically focusing on Dynamics 365 Dataverse connectivity and Enterprise Service Bus messaging patterns. * **Database Design:** Architect data layers using Cosmos DB (NoSQL) or SQL Server, ensuring optimal performance for transactional data stores. **Hands\-On Development*** **Core Engineering:** Write the critical path code. You will build the complex REST APIs, background services, and reusable front\-end components that the rest of the team will leverage. * **Full\-Stack Execution:** Work fluidly across the stack, developing responsive portals using Blazor/Razor or Angular, and connecting them to robust .NET C\# back\-ends. **Mentorship \& Quality*** **Technical Oversight:** Act as a technical advisor for a sub\-team of developers. You will review Pull Requests (PRs), enforce coding standards, and provide guidance to reduce technical blockers. * **CI/CD \& DevOps:** Develop and maintain robust CI/CD pipelines and unit test suites to ensure code reliability and smooth deployment to Azure. ### **Skills, Knowledge and Expertise** **Architectural Experience:** 8\-10 years of development experience, with at least 3\+ years acting in a Senior/Architect capacity.* **Microsoft Stack Mastery:** Expert\-level knowledge of .NET C\#, REST API development, and the Microsoft UI Technologies (Razor/Blazor) or Angular 10\+. * **Azure Depth:** Deep familiarity with Azure App Services, Function Apps, and Containerisation. * **Data Fluency:** Proficiency in designing for both NoSQL (Cosmos DB/Mongo) and Relational (SQL) databases. * **Integration Patterns:** Strong working knowledge of Enterprise Service Bus messaging patterns and experience integrating with platforms like Dynamics 365/Dataverse. **Location \& Eligibility** * **Hybrid Working:** Codec supports hybrid working; however, on\-site working will be required where expected by customers. * **Right to Work:** Candidates must be currently located in Ireland or the UK with existing Right to Work approval. ### **About Codec** Established over 40 years ago, Codec is a multi\-award\-winning Microsoft Solutions Partner, trusted by public and private sector organisations to deliver impactful digital transformation. With offices in **Dublin**, **Belfast**, **Cologne**, and across **Germany**, our team of over **300 experts** specialises in harnessing the full potential of Microsoft technologies—including **Dynamics 365**, **Power Platform**, **Azure**, and **Copilot**—to drive innovation, automation, and operational excellence. Codec combines deep industry expertise with a proven track record to help organisations unlock the power of AI and modern cloud solutions Though originally best known as a hardware reseller, since 2010 Codec has focused on building an outstanding software consulting business with a team of experts implementing enterprise solutions using products from Microsoft and Oracle. Our customer base, revenues and average contract values have grown steadily year on year and we continue to invest in the provision of new product and service offerings. Our team is a careful mix of recent graduates and experienced professionals from a variety of diverse cultural backgrounds. We are a melting pot of seasoned consultants supported by the best talent from the local universities. We have a thriving placement student academy and graduate recruitment programme which grows year on year. This company is an equal opportunities employer

You should be comfortable with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. * **Cloud Basics:** Exposure to **Azure** infrastructure (App Services, Azure Functions) is highly desirable. * **Database:** Experience with SQL or NoSQL databases. * **Testing:** Experience writing unit tests (e.g., xUnit, NUnit, Jasmine). **Location \& Eligibility** * **Hybrid Working:** Codec supports hybrid working; however, on\-site working will be required where expected by customers. * **Right to Work:** Candidates must be currently located in Ireland or the UK with existing Right to Work approval. ### **About Codec** Established over 40 years ago, Codec is a multi\-award\-winning Microsoft Solutions Partner, trusted by public and private sector organisations to deliver impactful digital transformation. title: Gastrointestinal Physiologist ; Chief II
description: **Job Description** **Job Title ;Gastrointestinal Physiologist Chief II** **Department ;Reflux Service** **Reports to ; Head of Endoscopy and Service Line Development** **Date ;2026** **Overall Purpose of Job** To deliver exceptional, patient\-centred care within an environment where quality, respect, caring, and compassion underpin all aspects of service delivery. The Gastrointestinal Physiologist Chief II is responsible for the investigation, interpretation, and reporting of gastrointestinal physiological function to support the diagnosis and management of motility disorders and functional gastrointestinal diseases. The post holder will be a senior and integral member of the Gastrointestinal team, with responsibility for advanced diagnostic testing, staff training and development, quality assurance, audit, and service development in line with hospital strategy and evolving clinical demand. **Key Responsibilities** ### **1\. Diagnostic Testing** Perform, interpret, and report specialised gastrointestinal physiology investigations, including but not limited to: o High\-resolution oesophageal manometry o 24\-hour pH and pH\-impedance monitoring o Small Intestinal Bacterial Overgrowth (SIBO) testing o Hydrogen and methane breath tests Prepare, calibrate, and maintain equipment to ensure accurate and reliable physiological measurements. Monitor patients throughout procedures to ensure safety, comfort, and dignity at all times. ### **2\. Clinical Duties** Support physicians in the diagnosis and management of gastrointestinal motility disorders (e.g. achalasia, gastroparesis). Explain procedures clearly to patients and obtain informed consent in accordance with hospital policy. Develop and maintain patient information leaflets to ensure patients are fully informed prior to investigations. Analyse diagnostic data and produce comprehensive, high\-quality clinical reports. Maintain accurate patient records and ensure compliance with data protection and confidentiality regulations. Actively contribute to innovation, service improvement, and implementation of best practice guidelines and emerging technologies. ### **3\. Education, Training, and Leadership** Provide training, supervision, and mentorship to junior staff, students, and new team members in GI physiology techniques. Support ongoing professional development within the department. Provide patient education regarding test preparation, procedures, and interpretation of results where appropriate. ### **4\. Quality Assurance and Service Development** Participate in audit, quality assurance, and service evaluation activities. Contribute to the development and expansion of GI physiology services in line with hospital objectives. Demonstrate a proactive approach to risk management, quality control, and continuous improvement. **Skills \& Competencies** In\-depth knowledge of gastrointestinal anatomy, physiology, and pathophysiology. Advanced technical expertise in motility and pH monitoring systems. Strong analytical, interpretative, and problem\-solving skills. Excellent communication skills to support effective patient interaction and multidisciplinary collaboration. High level of accuracy and attention to detail in clinical measurement and reporting. **Personal Specification** **Qualifications** * **Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Clinical Measurement Science,** Technological University Dublin (TUD) * **Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Clinical Measurement Science,** Dublin Institute of Technology (DIT) (up to 2018\) or equivalent as confirmed by the IICMP/HSE or * **Certificate in Medical Physics and Physiological Measurement (MPPM)** from Dublin Institute of Technology (*if qualified in or prior to 2005*). * Specialized training in GI physiology (may be gained through accredited courses or on\-the\-job training in a motility lab). **Experience** Minimum of 5 years' post\-qualification experience as a Gastrointestinal Physiologist. Broad and detailed knowledge of gastroenterology diagnostic testing. Demonstrated organisational and time management skills. Evidence of a proactive approach to quality improvement and service development. Management or supervisory experience is desirable. **Job Specific Teamwork Competencies and Knowledge** **Teamwork** Demonstrate ability to work as part of a multi\-disciplinary team. Demonstrate motivation and an innovative approach to job. **Knowledge \& Professional Knowledge** Demonstrate clinical competence and relevant experience. Demonstrate an ability to contribute to overall service development. **Planning \& Organisational Skills** Demonstrate organisational skills including effective time management. Demonstrate the ability to manage deadlines and effectively manage multiple tasks. **Communication \& Interpersonal Skills** Demonstrate effective communication skills including the ability to present information in a clear and concise manner. Demonstrate excellent communication skills especially the ability to communicate clear and concise instructions to patients in an understandable fashion. Demonstrate strong interpersonal skills. **Patient Focus** Demonstrate an awareness and appreciation of the service user. Demonstrate evidence of ability to empathise with and treat patients, relatives and colleagues with dignity and respect. **Personal Competencies** All posts in Beacon Hospital require a high level of flexibility to ensure the delivery of an effective and efficient service. Therefore, the post holder will be required to demonstrate flexibility as and when required by their manager or hospital management. This job description is intended to be an outline of the areas of responsibility and deliverables at the time of its writing. As the hospital and the post holder develop this job description may be subject to review in light of the changing needs of the hospital'

title: Graduate - Service Operations

The role provides structured exposure to, service delivery, operational processes, continuous improvement and technical problem solving, enabling graduates to develop the knowledge, skills and behaviours required to support safe, reliable and efficient customer operations. Specific Skills: * Technical aptitude – ability to learn mechanical, electrical and hydraulic systems * Problem‑solving – structured approach to fault finding and diagnosis * Analytical thinking – ability to interpret data and identify trends/opportunities * Process improvement mindset – identifying inefficiencies and suggesting practical solutions * Commercial awareness – understanding impact of service performance on cost and customer outcomes * Manual and practical skills – working safely with tools, equipment and components * Attention to detail – following procedures and technical standards accurately, as well as identifying potential for efficiency through inventions and process/control updates * Communication – explaining technical issues clearly to supervisors and team members * Teamwork – working effectively within workshop and field\-based teams * Safety awareness – understanding and applying safe systems of work Knowledge: * Foundational understanding of description: **Company:** Finning (UK) Ltd **Number of Openings:** 1 **Worker Type:** Permanent **Position Overview:** The Finning Service \- Operations Graduate role is designed to build operational, analytical and technical capability and develop future service, engineering and technical leaders within the organisation. The role provides structured exposure to, service delivery, operational processes, continuous improvement and technical problem solving, enabling graduates to develop the knowledge, skills and behaviours required to support safe, reliable and efficient customer operations. Service / technical Graduates will gain experience supporting the end to end service lifecycle, whilst also identifying, analysing and implementing improvement processes, systems and ways of working to drive efficiency and customer outcomes. Depending on business needs, the role may include more technically focused work or more operational / process\-focused work **Job Description:** Major Job Functions: * Learn and apply mechanical, electrical or electronic engineering skills on Caterpillar and associated equipment * Lead or support structured improvement projects focused on efficiency, cost reduction and service performance * Support planned maintenance, inspection and servicing activities under supervision * Assist with fault diagnosis, troubleshooting and repair activities using approved procedures * Use diagnostic tools, systems and technical documentation to support equipment reliability * Analyse service processes and identify opportunities to improve efficiency, quality and customer experience * Support continuous improvement initiatives (e.g. Lean, waste reduction, standardisation, digital process improvements) * Collect and interpret operational data to support decision\-making and performance improvement * Work collaboratively with experienced engineers, supervisors and technical specialists * Maintain accurate service records, reports and documentation * Actively engage in technical training, safety programmes, coaching and performance feedback * Demonstrate Finning values and safety leadership in all workshop and site activities Competencies * Create Safe Environments: Focuses on creating and contributing to a diverse, inclusive, and positive workplace where people are motivated to do their best. * Build Loyalty: Focuses on building strong relationships to deliver common goals. It is about continuously paying attention to customer needs and adapting as these evolve. * Ensure Accountability: Focuses on transparency and reliability, ensuring goals are met by holding self and others accountable for meeting commitments and maintaining high standards. * Prepare and Deliver: Focuses on effective planning, resource management and prioritizing work to meet commitments aligned with the company goals. Specific Skills: * Technical aptitude – ability to learn mechanical, electrical and hydraulic systems * Problem‑solving – structured approach to fault finding and diagnosis * Analytical thinking – ability to interpret data and identify trends/opportunities * Process improvement mindset – identifying inefficiencies and suggesting practical solutions * Commercial awareness – understanding impact of service performance on cost and customer outcomes * Manual and practical skills – working safely with tools, equipment and components * Attention to detail – following procedures and technical standards accurately, as well as identifying potential for efficiency through inventions and process/control updates * Communication – explaining technical issues clearly to supervisors and team members * Teamwork – working effectively within workshop and field\-based teams * Safety awareness – understanding and applying safe systems of work Knowledge: * Foundational understanding of mechanical, electrical or electronic engineering principles * Awareness of preventative maintenance and inspection processes * Understanding of fault diagnosis principles and root cause analysis (developed during role) * Awareness of service operations processes and performance metrics (e.g. utilisation, turnaround time, cost efficiency) * Basic understanding of continuous improvement methodologies (Lean, problem\-solving frameworks, etc.) * Awareness of the importance of equipment reliability, uptime and safety for customers * Understanding of how service quality impacts customer satisfaction and operational performance * Ability to work through problems systematically and propose and implement changes that improve efficiency and/or customer experience Accountability: * Supporting planned maintenance and service activities in line with schedules and procedures * Ensuring work is carried out safely, complying with Finning and site safety standards * Identifying and escalating opportunities to improve processes, efficiency and customer outcomes * Contributing to continuous improvement initiatives within the Service function * Accurate completion of job cards, inspection reports and service documentation * Escalating issues, faults or risks appropriately to supervisors * Maintaining a professional approach when interacting with colleagues and customers * Supporting projects that enhance customer experience Education \& Experience: Completed undergraduate degree (Level 6 or equivalent) in a relevant technical discipline such as: * Mechanical Engineering * Electrical or Electronic Engineering * Mechatronics * Automotive, Plant, Marine or Heavy Equipment Engineering * Engineering Technology or similar technical field Or an equivalent qualification that provides strong engineering fundamentals aligned to service operations. **Experience** * No prior full‑time professional service engineering experience required. * Experience gained through academic study, industrial placements, workshops, apprenticeships, part‑time or voluntary roles is sufficient where it demonstrates one or more of the following: + Exposure to hands‑on mechanical or technical work + Application of safe working practices + Use of tools, equipment or diagnostic systems + Ability to follow procedures and technical instructions + Understanding of operational discipline and teamwork If you would like to be a part of an industry leading company offering career development then apply online today At Finning, we prioritize creating a diverse and inclusive environment. As a company, we continuously strive to enhance our outreach to individuals of all backgrounds and identities. title: Graduate Electrical Engineer – Power and Renewables
description: Graduate Electrical Engineer \- Power \& Renewables **About Kirby Group Engineering** Kirby Group Engineering is a leading international engineering services contractor, delivering Mechanical, Electrical and Instrumentation solutions across Ireland, the UK and Europe. We work with major clients in fast‑growing sectors including power transmission, renewables, data centres and life sciences, and are known for our strong safety culture, technical excellence and focus on developing people. **Power \& Renewables at Kirby** Kirby Is a Market Leader In Power And Renewables, Delivering HV And MV Design And Build Projects Up To 400kV, Including AIS And GIS Substations, Grid Connections And Renewable Infrastructure. Our Teams Work On * HV/MV substations and grid connections * HVDC interconnector projects * Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) * Electrical and civil Balance of Plant works * We have connected over 2GW of renewable energy to the grid in Ireland and the UK and deliver projects for organisations such as ESB Networks, EirGrid, TSOs, wind developers and major industrial clients. **The Graduate Programme** The Kirby Electrical Engineering Graduate Programme is a 24‑month programme designed to kick‑start your career in Power \& Renewables. **You Will** * * Be supported by a dedicated mentor * Work on live HV, MV and renewables projects * Complete structured rotations across Engineering, EHS, Quality, Planning and Commercial teams * Receive high‑quality technical, personal and commercial training through the Kirby Academy * On successful completion, graduates progress to Electrical Project Engineer. **The Role** As a Graduate Electrical Engineer in Power \& Renewables, you will support the delivery of electrical works on major infrastructure projects. You’ll gain hands‑on site experience while developing your engineering, safety and commercial awareness. **What You’ll Be Involved In** * Supporting electrical installation works on HV/MV and renewables projects * Reviewing drawings, specifications and technical submissions * Assisting with site inspections, snagging and coordination * Supporting procurement, material tracking and supplier engagement * Participating in testing, commissioning and energisation activities * Maintaining quality, safety and progress documentation * Working closely with project teams, subcontractors and clients **Essential** **What We’re Looking For** * Level 8 Degree in Electrical Engineering or related discipline * Good understanding of electrical engineering fundamentals * Ability to read and interpret technical drawings *Desirable* * Internship or placement experience * Interest in power systems, renewables, substations or energy infrastructure * Familiarity with AutoCAD **About You** * Motivated, curious and keen to learn * Strong problem‑solving and communication skills * Enjoy working as part of a team * Organised, professional and safety‑focused

title: HR Executive
description: **HR Executive (12 month Fixed Term Contract)** ***Our vision is to make net zero a reality by solving the commercial mechanics of a decarbonised energy system.*** ElectroRoute is an international, renewables focused, energy trading and services company, established in 2011\. In 2016, we became part of the Mitsubishi Corporation family, who have brought with them a wealth of innovation and global energy experience which is allowing us to enhance and grow our client base and service offerings. ElectroRoute provides a comprehensive range of energy trading and market services designed to support the transition to a more sustainable energy system. By combining deep expertise in energy markets with cutting\-edge digital infrastructure, we help our clients maximise the value of their renewable assets, navigate market volatility, and contribute to system\-wide decarbonisation. At the core of ElectroRoute’s success is our people. Their dedicated commitment to our corporate values of Excellence, Curiosity, and Humanity underpins every decision we make and serves to enhance the outcomes created on behalf of our clients. As we look to the future, our focus remains clear: to scale our impact, deepen our partnerships, and continue innovating for a carbon free system. We are very proud to be on this mission; will you join us? **The Position** **Payroll Processing / Benefit Management** * You will be responsible for the accurate, timely, and compliant delivery of payroll across designated countries or entities, acting as a key payroll specialist who will own end\-to\-end payroll processing which includes: including the review and validation of payroll inputs and outputs, coordination of payroll calendars and statutory deadlines and the processing of all payroll changes such as new hires, terminations, salary changes, bonuses, benefits, leaves. * Prepare and validate payroll submissions for our payroll providers * Completing salary analysis and preparing journals for finance. * Ensure full compliance with payroll legislation, including tax, pensions, BIK, and other statutory obligations * Maintain accurate and up\-to\-date payroll and employee records on payroll and HRIS. * Respond to payroll queries from employees, providing clear explanations and timely resolutions * Work closely with HR and Finance teams to ensure seamless payroll integration and data accuracy * Carry out payroll audits and investigate and resolve discrepancies * Administration, reconciliation and payment of third\-party payroll deductions to relevant pension and healthcare providers * Completion of forms relating to Department of Social Protection and mortgage applications **HR Executive** * Support and promote the company culture by championing organisational values across the business. * Respond promptly and professionally to employee HR queries. * Coordinate recruitment activities for internship, temporary, and permanent roles. * Support the onboarding process for new hires, including contract preparation and ensuring a smooth transition into the organisation. * Administer employee benefits, including healthcare, pension, and related schemes. * Prepare and issue employee documentation across all stages of the employment lifecycle. * Maintain accurate HR records, reporting, and documentation within BambooHR * Provide general HR administrative support, including employee letters and document management. * Coordinate internal company communications and HR\-related sessions as required. * Manage recruitment administration, including job advertising, agency coordination, interview scheduling, and interview support. * Support employees with visa and work permit administration where required. * Assist in delivering employee wellbeing initiatives and engagement activities. * Deliver induction training, performance management training, and other HR training programmes. * Manage relationships with external HR vendors and service providers. * Work collaboratively with the HR team to foster employee engagement, team spirit, and a positive workplace culture, including supporting company events and celebrations. **The Successful Candidate should be able to demonstrate the following:** * Minimum of 3 – 5 years HR or payroll experience * Third level qualification in Payroll, HR, Business or related discipline is desirable. * Good planning and organisational skills * High level of accuracy and exceptional attention to detail * Ability to work effectively under pressure and meet strict deadlines * Proficient in MS Office, particularly Excel * Strong administrative and document management skills * Use of Bamboo system or other HRIS * Flexible and eager to learn material in new fields and develop new work practices * Ability to work independently while contributing positively as part of a team **Personal Attributes:** * **Proactive and Independent**: Ability to take initiative and work autonomously while managing various tasks. * **Professionalism**: A professional attitude and approach, both internally with colleagues and externally with clients and vendors. * **Adaptability**: Flexibility to adapt to changing environments and manage unforeseen challenges. * **Confidentiality**: Ability to handle sensitive information discreetly and professionally. **Location** The role will be based in Dublin and will be hybrid (3 days in the office) **Remuneration** Competitive package with a market competitive bonus structure. **Diversity \& Inclusion** * ElectroRoute is an equal opportunity employer. We aim to create value through diversity and inclusion. We seek to attract candidates to reflect the diverse nature of our society. Diversity and inclusion allow the ElectroRoute team to be curious, creative, and promote positive change. We understand the many demands on all our employees, and we adopt flexible working arrangements where possible. Working at ElectroRoute offers something special for everyone. Pay: €40,000\.00\-€55,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company events * Company pension * Employee assistance program * Sick pay * Work from home Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: Hybrid remote in DUBLIN 2, County Dublin

title: Hotel Management Graduate Programme
description: **Ready to kick\-start your career in hospitality?** Our Manager in Training (MIT) Graduate Programme commences in September and is your chance to step into leadership with a structured 18\-month journey designed to build your confidence, skills and future. What you'll gain: * The opportunity to learn directly from experienced leaders across the business * A comprehensive understanding of hotel operations * Exposure to real challenges and responsibilities in a supportive setting * A clear pathway to develop your career with structured guidance and mentorship. * Click on the link below to find out more: https://online.flippingbook.com/view/541185829/ Who we are looking for: * Due to graduate or recently graduated with a relevant degree (Hospitality or Tourism \- however if you do not have a degree in these fields, but have experience in hospitality, we would still love to hear from you) * Excellent communication, teamwork and interpersonal skills * Ability to adapt to a fast paced environment * Passion for hospitality, guest satisfaction * Willingness to work flexible hours, including evenings and weekends * Open to travel within Ireland for the hotel rotations * Those seeking operational roles within the hotel industry (F\&B, Rooms, Front Office, M\&E and Reservations) If you are motivated, eager to learn and ready to take on new challenges, this programme will provide you with the tools and experience to succeed as a future manager. From the bustling city centres of Dublin, Galway, Limerick and Belfast , to picturesque and historic estates close to Wicklow and Galway City, each of our hotels has its own distinct personality and charm. *Due to the high volume of applications received, we regret that we are only able to contact candidates whose experience and profile closely match the requirements of the role. We sincerely thank all applicants for their interest and for taking the time to apply*

title: IT Business Analyst - Medical Underwriting (French)
description: **Company Description** PartnerRe is a leading, privately owned, multi\-line global reinsurer with a reputation of financial stability and strength, and a commitment to rebuilding businesses and communities after risk events around the world. Our mission is to continue to be a financially stable and predictable business partner, supporting our clients with solutions tailored to their needs, delivering superior returns to our owner, providing rewarding opportunities for our people and making a positive contribution to society. To achieve this, we live by our values of Integrity, Performance, Collaboration, Straightforward Communication and Respect and Care. Core to The PartnerRe Experience is our global nature. No matter where you sit in the company, you are part of something bigger. Our teams represent our truly international mindset, forging connections across office locations and all levels of the organization and building a network of mutual success. The result is a day\-to\-day experience that will allow you to: **Shape your own career** **Grow in a supportive environment** **Connect with a community of experts** **Make an impact** View Home \- PartnerRe Experience e\-book to find out more about what it’s like to work at PartnerRe. **We are always looking for bright, proactive individuals to join our team!** Please follow our Careers page for all updates on new positions. We look forward to receiving your application. **PartnerRe is an equal opportunities employer.** Please join our company LinkedIn Page for all updates on new positions that are coming live. **Compensation And Benefits** The expected base salary range for this position, when hired into our **Toronto** location, is CAD **$93,713 – $114,538** per year. For positions based in other jurisdictions, compensation will be determined in alignment with local regulations, market benchmarks, and internal compensation frameworks. The above range reflects the typical base salary range for candidates hired into this position. The actual salary will vary depending on a number of factors, including job location, experience, qualifications, education/training, skills, market conditions, and alignment with the role’s requirements. In addition to base salary, eligible employees may participate in our annual incentive program, payment under which is at the discretion of PartnerRe and subject to individual and company performance. Eligible employees also have the opportunity to participate in an array of benefits, including medical, dental and vision coverage, a health spending account, a defined contribution pension plan, life insurance, short\- and long\-term disability coverage, etc. **Working Arrangement** PartnerRe operates with a hybrid work approach that balances in‑office collaboration with the flexibility of remote work. Our employees are required to be in the office 3 days a week. Our goal is to create an environment where employees can connect, innovate, and perform at their best. This position is not for an existing vacancy within our organization. PartnerRe is committed to an inclusive, accessible recruitment experience. Reasonable accommodations for candidates with disabilities are available upon request throughout the hiring process. **Job Summary** We are looking for an IT Business Analyst to join our Group IT Life and Health Underwriting and Claims application team and manage the development and operations of the medical underwriting manuals and market solutions within PartnerRe. This role will be based in Dublin OR Toronto, working with our internal business stakeholders (e.g., medical underwriters, medical consultants, client partners) and IT teams on the global level to ensure that our IT solutions effectively support the complex business and compliance requirements of our Life and Health reinsurance business. **About The Role** * Act as an IT BA to manage the application SDLC \& ITSM processes as well as user related production support and change requests for PartnerRe’s medical underwriting manual platform (PAR) as well as automated underwriting platform (Duet Edge). * Work with business product owners, global medical underwriting SMEs and suppliers to manage the content review cycle and version control of the medical underwriting manual platform to ensure consistency, accuracy, and up\-to\-date medical underwriting guidelines. * Updating and reviewing rules in the automated underwriting platform to reflect changes in our underwriting philosophy. This platform is separate from the underwriting manual platform. * Manage internal and external user on/off boarding as well as assist the rollout of application upgrades to existing users/clients/markets. * Drive the design, validation, implementation and quality assurance of potential new IT solutions in collaboration with architecture, development, and business teams. **Qualifications** **Technical Skills:** * Proven experience in application life cycle management and IT service management of HTML, CSS, Content version control tools, and workflow tools. * Proficiency with BA tools (e.g., Azure DevOps, Asana, O365\) and agile methodologies (Scrum). * Proven experience in requirements gathering, process modelling, and solution design **Soft Skills** * Good stakeholder, client, supplier management and communication skills in both English and French. * Proactive problem\-solving, resilience in the face of challenges, and an optimistic, action\-oriented mindset **Work Experience** * 5\+ years of experience as an IT Business Analyst ideally with a focus on Life \& Health medical underwriting. **Education/Professional Qualifications** * Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Information Technology, Business Administration, Insurance, or a related field. * Scrum Master, Project Management and ITSM related certification are desired. * Fluent French language skills are required. **Additional Information** : PartnerRe provides a working environment in which diversity is valued, where all employees feel welcome, respected, and empowered to perform at their best. We invite you to learn more about D\&I at PartnerRe. Thank you, PartnerRe Hiring Team

title: IT Logistics
description: **Company Overview** Milestone Technologies is a global IT managed services firm that partners with organizations to scale their technology, infrastructure and services to drive specific business outcomes such as digital transformation, innovation, and operational agility. Milestone is focused on building an employee\-first, performance\-based culture and for over 25 years, we have a demonstrated history of supporting category\-defining enterprise clients that are growing ahead of the market. The company specializes in providing solutions across Application Services and Consulting, Digital Product Engineering, Digital Workplace Services, Private Cloud Services, AI/Automation, and ServiceNow. Milestone culture is built to provide a collaborative, inclusive environment that supports employees and empowers them to reach their full potential. Our seasoned professionals deliver services based on Milestone’s best practices and service delivery framework. By leveraging our vast knowledge base to execute initiatives, we deliver both short\-term and long\-term value to our clients and apply continuous service improvement to deliver transformational benefits to IT. With Intelligent Automation, Milestone helps businesses further accelerate their IT transformation. The result is a sharper focus on business objectives and a dramatic improvement in employee productivity. Through our key technology partnerships and our people\-first approach, Milestone continues to deliver industry\-leading innovation to our clients. With more than 3,000 employees serving over 200 companies worldwide, we are following our mission of revolutionizing the way IT is deployed. **Job Overview** Milestone is looking for a skilled IT Asset Logistics Technician to provide onsite services for our client supporting a global team. In this role, you will be engaged with inventory management, imaging and deploying of Windows and macOS laptops, mobile devices, and the essential equipment that keeps our client and their teams going. **How You Will Make a Difference** * New Hire equipment ticket management * Imaging and deployment of laptops, peripherals, and equipment * Manage and process delivery and deployment of logistics request tickets * Process and record received deliveries of existing equipment * Confirm and validate new asset receipts of hardware received * Escalate, report, and manage discrepancies between hardware assets physically received * Conduct weekly stockroom inventory asset audits * Collect, process, and manage legal hold assets * Monitor and report inventory stock levels in accordance with established thresholds * Maintain and track hardware to and from the inventory store rooms * Perform hardware recovery upon employee terminations and laptop replacements * Identify disposition of returned assets * Assist to arrange and manage e\-waste services **What You Will Need To Succeed** * HS Diploma or equivalent * Minimum 2 year of Inventory Management experience * Ability to work in an onsite capacity following Milestone Technologies and Client safety guidelines * Extensive understanding of IT equipment, models, and accessories including Windows, macOS, Android, and iOS devices * Excellent ticket management hygiene * Responsive and efficient communication via email, Slack, and Zoom; exhibiting a high level of professionalism * Knowledge of storeroom safety and operations * Ability to lift/move 25\-50 pounds * Strong verbal and written skills for customer interactions * Minimum 2 years of imaging, and deployment experience * Knowledge and experience using ServiceNow, JAMF, BigFix, and other device management/service delivery software **Compensation** Estimated Pay Range: Exact compensation and offers of employment are dependent on circumstances of each case and will be determined based on job\-related knowledge, skills, experience, licenses or certifications, and location. ****Our Commitment to Diversity \& Inclusion**** At Milestone we strive to create a workplace that reflects the communities we serve and work with, where we all feel empowered to bring our full, authentic selves to work. We know creating a diverse and inclusive culture that champions equity and belonging is not only the right thing to do for our employees but is also critical to our continued success. Milestone Technologies provides equal employment opportunity for all applicants and employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment and will not be discriminated against on the basis of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity, marital status, age, disability, veteran status, sexual orientation, national origin, or any other category protected by applicable federal and state law, or local ordinance. Milestone also makes reasonable accommodations for disabled applicants and employees. We welcome the unique background, culture, experiences, knowledge, innovation, self\-expression and perspectives you can bring to our global community. Our recruitment team is looking forward to meeting you. **\*\*\*Notice to Job Seekers Regarding Impersonation and Fraudulent Job Offers\*\*\*** It has come to our attention that unauthorized individuals and entities are impersonating Milestone Technologies, Inc. on various job boards/portals, **specifically Naukri.com and Linkedin.com** **Please be advised of the following official Milestone Technologies recruitment policies:** **Official Communication Channels** All legitimate email communications from our recruitment team will originate ONLY from the domains: **@milestone.tech.** We do not use generic email services (e.g. @gmail.com, @yahoo.com, @outlook.com) or unofficial third party messaging apps to conduct formal business. **No Recruitment Fees** Milestone Technologies never requests any form of payment, "security deposit", "laptop/equipment fee", or "processing fee" at any stage of the recruitment or onboarding process. Any request for money in exchange for an interview or job offer is a scam.

title: IT Service Desk Technician
description: At John Sisk \& Son, we’re more than a construction company, we’re a family\-owned business with a global reach, committed to building excellence in every project. We deliver trust, certainty and value to our clients while embracing innovation and shaping the future of construction. The Service Desk Technician role is responsible for the customer facing and 1st line support role within the Service Desk, on a day\-to\-day basis and to provide support and feedback to the Management \& team coordinator in the running of the department. The role also ensures that incidents and requests are processed within our service level agreements and resolved to customer satisfaction. * Provide 1st level response to technical software and hardware incidents for all Group users, to agreed service levels both in terms of performance and customer satisfaction. * Liaise with 2nd level technical support staff to ensure support response times are within agreed limits * Installation and configuration of approved hardware, software and mobile devices * Creating, updating and flagging knowledge base articles as required * Clearly communicate technical solutions in a user friendly and professional manner * Keep users informed of call status if requested. * Site attendance as required * Delivers, Tags, sets up and assists in the configuration of end user PC hardware, software and peripherals. * Co\-ordinates timely repair of PC equipment covered by 3rd party vendor maintenance agreements. * Escalation of issues to the Service Desk Management team * Desktop Operating Systems * Basic hardware for PC’s * Basic Active Directory preferred * Basic Office 365 administration * Service Management system * A\+ certification\-preferred * MCSA/MCITP certification desirable * ITIL Foundation\-preferred * Competitive salary * 26 days annual leave plus additional company days * Pension and benefits * Unlimited learning and development opportunities * Much more **Our People and Recruitment Commitment** At Sisk Group our people are at the heart of our success. We offer the chance to work with purpose, build a career with no limits and be part of a great team. All vacancies are managed directly by our Internal Recruitment Team. Unsolicited contact or speculative CVs from unapproved agencies will not be accepted and no introductory fees will apply. We are an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, race, ethnicity, disability, age, sexual orientation, religion or any other protected characteristic. Reasonable adjustments are available during the recruitment process. We know the confidence gap and imposter phenomenon can stop talented candidates applying. You do not need to meet every criterion \- your skills and potential matter. Don’t hold back we want to hear from you. All recruitment materials comply with legal and regulatory requirements including the EU Pay Transparency Directive and the Equality Acts UK and Ireland.

title: IT Support & Projects Engineer
description: My client based in Naas, are seeking a proactive and forward\-thinking IT professional to join their clients team in a hybrid role that combines vendor management, deskside support, project delivery, and emerging technology leadership. 80\-90% of the role will be vendor management. title: IT Support Engineer L1/L2 - MSP
description: **Level 1 / Level 2 IT Support Engineer – MSP** **South County Dublin \| 5 days office\-based \| MSP experience required** A small, well\-established MSP in South County Dublin is hiring a Level 1 / Level 2 IT Support Engineer to join its service desk team. This role is for someone with around 3\-4\+ years’ IT support experience gained in an MSP environment. MSP experience is required, as you will be supporting multiple clients, managing ticket flow, switching between different environments, and troubleshooting at pace. This is a 5 days per week office\-based role. This is non\-negotiable. You must already be living in South County Dublin, and you must be fully legal to work in Ireland now. There is no visa sponsorship and no relocation support for this role. Most of the work will be service desk based, supporting clients remotely, but there may be occasional site visits when needed. This is not a field engineer role, but you will need to be presentable, client\-focused, and comfortable visiting client sites from time to time. **What you’ll be doing** * Managing and resolving support tickets across multiple clients * Troubleshooting Windows desktop, user, application and connectivity issues * Supporting Microsoft 365, Entra ID / Active Directory and user account issues * Helping users with MFA, Authenticator apps and access\-related problems * Basic network troubleshooting across TCP/IP, DNS and related issues * Supporting security tools and processes such as EDR, MDR and general cyber security hygiene * Communicating clearly with clients by phone, email and ticket updates * Escalating issues properly when needed * Carrying out occasional client site visits when required **What you’ll need** * Around **4\+ years’ IT support experience** * Previous **MSP experience** — this is required * Strong Level 1 support ability, with some Level 2 troubleshooting experience * Good Windows troubleshooting experience * Good understanding of TCP/IP, DNS and basic networking * Experience with Active Directory and/or Entra ID * Exposure to Microsoft 365 * Awareness of MFA, Authenticator apps, EDR/MDR and general cyber security basics * Strong written and verbal communication skills * A positive, can\-do attitude and willingness to keep learning * Good troubleshooting instincts and the ability to move quickly between issues * You must be living in **South County Dublin** * Access to your own car or motorbike would be very useful, but it is not essential This role would suit someone who enjoys the variety of MSP work, wants exposure to different clients and technologies, and is happy working in a small, flexible team. Apply with your CV if you experience as a Technical Support Engineer and your location fits the requirements.

title: Installation Technician III
